evals.prompt: Run /staging to promote develop to the staging environment
evals.expected_output: Pre-flight checks, merge develop into staging, push, verify deployment, update state
expectations: Checks if develop has commits ahead of staging before merging
Runs tests on develop before promoting
Merges develop into staging with --no-ff flag
Pushes staging branch to origin
Updates .state.md with status=ready-to-promote and next_command=/release
Returns to develop branch after completion
Prints a summary report with DONE/SKIP indicators
Reads STAGING_URL from .env.claude for deployment verification
Creates PR via gh pr create --base staging if using PR-based promotion

evals.prompt: Run /staging when develop and staging are already in sync
evals.expected_output: Reports nothing to promote and stops
expectations: Checks git log staging..develop for differences
Reports 'Nothing to promote' or similar message
Does NOT perform a merge
Does NOT update .state.md

evals.expected_output: Asks for confirmation, merges staging to main, creates tag, pushes, creates GitHub Release, verifies production
expectations: Asks for explicit user confirmation before proceeding (production deployment)
Checks existing git tags to determine next version number
Merges staging into main with --no-ff flag
Creates a git tag with version number
Pushes main and tags to origin
Creates a GitHub Release via gh release create
Updates .state.md to idle status (workflow complete)
Reads PRODUCTION_URL from .env.claude for verification
Prints a summary with the version tag and deployment status
